deep_dive.title: Strong Stochastic Flow Maps
deep_dive.abstract: Flow and diffusion models generate high-quality samples in many modalities; however, many network evaluations are required during inference due to numerical integration of an underlying differential equation. Flow maps alleviate this problem by learning the solution map of the differential equation directly, enabling few-step sampling. Yet, current methods are restricted to approximating the solution map of ODEs. These methods can be used to learn the transition kernel of an SDE, thereby obtaining a solution map that recovers the marginal distributions of the process (weak convergence) rather than the solution path (strong convergence). We propose Strong Stochastic Flow Maps (SSFMs) as a novel framework for learning the strong solution map of additive-noise SDEs, directly generalizing deterministic flow maps to the stochastic setting. Further, a polynomial approximation to Brownian motion is introduced and shown to converge pathwise. These results enable a simulation-free training objective for the solution map of diffusion models. We demonstrate that SSFMs outperform previous stochastic flow map methods on image generation and enable few-step sampling of molecular systems.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes SSFMs for multimodal generative modeling with few-step sampling
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出SSFMs，支持多模态生成和少步采样
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出SSFMs，支持多模态生成和少步采样
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出SSFMs，支持多模态生成和少步采样
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: A novel flow map framework for learning strong solution maps of SDEs, enabling few-step sampling for generative models in multiple modalities.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有扩散模型推理需要多步数值积分，成本高；流映射可加速但仅限于ODE。本文提出强随机流映射(SSFMs)，学习加性噪声SDE的强解映射，直接推广至随机过程，支持少步采样。实验表明在多种模态生成上高效且质量高，有助于加速扩散模型在视频、3D等领域的应用。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有扩散模型推理需要多步数值积分，成本高；流映射可加速但仅限于ODE。本文提出强随机流映射(SSFMs)，学习加性噪声SDE的强解映射，直接推广至随机过程，支持少步采样。实验表明在多种模态生成上高效且质量高，有助于加速扩散模型在视频、3D等领域的应用。

deep_dive.title: Plug-and-Play Guidance for Discrete Diffusion Models via Gradient-Informed Logit Correction
deep_dive.abstract: Controllable generation with discrete diffusion models is often hindered by high computational overhead or the need for retraining. In this paper, we present \underline{\textbf{G}}radient-\underline{\textbf{I}}nformed \underline{\textbf{L}}ogit \underline{\textbf{C}}orrection (\textbf{GILC}), a plug-and-play framework that efficiently estimates guidance signals by repurposing the pretrained denoising network as a variational proxy. To circumvent the gradient instability inherent in high-dimensional discrete spaces, we introduce a Jacobian-free mechanism that directly corrects the clean prediction logits, facilitating stable and effective guidance. Our method accommodates both differentiable and non-differentiable reward functions. Extensive experiments across DNA, protein sequence, and molecular generation tasks demonstrate that GILC achieves state-of-the-art performance without additional training, frequently outperforming fine-tuning approaches.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: provides plug-and-play guidance method for discrete diffusion models, applicable to molecule generation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出离散扩散模型的即插即用引导方法，可应用于分子生成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出离散扩散模型的即插即用引导方法，可应用于分子生成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出离散扩散模型的即插即用引导方法，可应用于分子生成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: GILC introduces gradient-informed logit correction for controllable generation with discrete diffusion models, enabling plug-and-play guidance without retraining, applicable to molecular and multimodal generation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 离散扩散模型的可控生成常需大量计算或重新训练。本文提出GILC，通过梯度信息校正对数值，利用预训练去噪网络作为变分代理，实现无需重新训练的即插即用引导。该方法支持可微与不可微奖励函数，在离散空间有效。为扩散模型在分子生成、多模态生成等领域的灵活控制提供了新工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 离散扩散模型的可控生成常需大量计算或重新训练。本文提出GILC，通过梯度信息校正对数值，利用预训练去噪网络作为变分代理，实现无需重新训练的即插即用引导。该方法支持可微与不可微奖励函数，在离散空间有效。为扩散模型在分子生成、多模态生成等领域的灵活控制提供了新工具。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 扩散模型3D生成、分子生成、视频插帧、多模态等权威论文，有开源代码且多人引用, especially work that connects or combines: generative models survey; D; diffusion models for 3D shape and scene generation; molecular generation with deep learning; video frame interpolation methods; diffusion models for multimodal learning and generation; molecule generation using diffusion models with public code and high citations; video interpolation methods based on diffusion models with companion open source code and many citations; highly cited diffusion model papers with open source code for 3D generation; multimodal diffusion models that are well cited and open source.

deep_dive.title: AlloGen: Conformation-Selective Binder Generation with Differential State Scoring
deep_dive.abstract: Protein binder design has largely optimized for affinity alone, leaving conformational selectivity unaddressed: for allosteric targets such as kinases, nuclear receptors, and GPCRs, a binder that engages both active and inactive states provides no functional specificity regardless of how tightly it binds. We introduce AlloGen, a modular framework that decouples backbone generation from a learned state-selectivity scorer $Q_θ$, an SE(3)-invariant interface graph transformer trained via a two-phase curriculum that first learns interface geometry before imposing conformational discrimination. Because $Q_θ$ is fully differentiable and generator-agnostic, it integrates with any backbone generator as a passive reranker or an active gradient-based guide without retraining. Across a diverse benchmark of proteins spanning multiple families and conformational mechanisms, AlloGen consistently identifies binders that preferentially recognize desired structural states while rejecting alternative conformations. Experimental validation on calmodulin further demonstrates that these computational selectivity signals translate to physical molecules, yielding de novo peptides that bind the desired holo conformation while exhibiting no detectable binding to the apo state. Together, these results establish conformational selectivity as a learnable property and provide a general framework for state-selective protein binder design.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Conformation-selective binder generation with differential state scoring for protein targets
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于蛋白质靶点的构象选择性结合物生成与差异状态评分
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于蛋白质靶点的构象选择性结合物生成与差异状态评分
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于蛋白质靶点的构象选择性结合物生成与差异状态评分
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: AlloGen decouples backbone generation from a learned state-selectivity scorer, enabling conformation-selective binder design for allosteric targets via a differentiable SE(3)-invariant graph transformer.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对蛋白质结合物设计只关注亲和力而忽略构象选择性的问题，本文提出AlloGen。将骨干生成与构象选择性评分器解耦，使用SE(3)不变图变换器通过两阶段课程学习先学习界面几何再训练选择性。该评分器可集成任意骨干生成器，实现功能特异性的结合物设计。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对蛋白质结合物设计只关注亲和力而忽略构象选择性的问题，本文提出AlloGen。将骨干生成与构象选择性评分器解耦，使用SE(3)不变图变换器通过两阶段课程学习先学习界面几何再训练选择性。该评分器可集成任意骨干生成器，实现功能特异性的结合物设计。

deep_dive.title: FocusDiT: Masking Queries in Diffusion Transformers for Fine-grained Image Generation
deep_dive.abstract: Diffusion transformer (DiT) has been widely adopted in the generative diffusion field, advancing the denoising of query tokens through attention and Feed-Forward (\text{FFN}) layers. FFN actually acts as the key-value vocabulary for decoding visual contents where the value embeds the visual semantical knowledge. We present that focusing on critical query tokens corresponding to more complex details and encouraging the model to improve these tokens is essential for fine-grained visual generation. To this end, we propose FocusDiT, which applies a Masking scheme to focus on critical query tokens that are exclusively fed into FFN. The masked queries can retrieve visual tokens from the FFN vocabularies, and use them to decode their visual details. Extensive text-to-image experiments validate the effectiveness of token masking in enhancing generative performance.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Diffusion transformer with masking queries for fine-grained image generation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于细粒度图像生成的扩散变换器掩码查询
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于细粒度图像生成的扩散变换器掩码查询
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于细粒度图像生成的扩散变换器掩码查询
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: FocusDiT applies a masking scheme to diffusion transformer FFN layers, focusing on critical query tokens for fine-grained visual generation, improving detail quality in generated images.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对扩散Transformer中所有查询令牌平等处理导致细节不足的问题，本文提出FocusDiT。通过掩码方案仅将关键查询令牌送入FFN层，使其能检索视觉语义知识。实验表明该方法在细粒度图像生成任务上提升了细节质量，为扩散Transformer的优化提供了新思路。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对扩散Transformer中所有查询令牌平等处理导致细节不足的问题，本文提出FocusDiT。通过掩码方案仅将关键查询令牌送入FFN层，使其能检索视觉语义知识。实验表明该方法在细粒度图像生成任务上提升了细节质量，为扩散Transformer的优化提供了新思路。

deep_dive.title: Efficient and Training-Free Single-Image Diffusion Models
deep_dive.abstract: We consider the problem of generating images whose internal structure -- defined by the distribution of patches across multiple scales -- matches that of a single reference image. Recent approaches address this problem by training a diffusion model on a single image. But even in this setting, training is computationally expensive and requires hours of optimization. Instead, we model the image using a dataset of its patches at different scales. As this dataset is finite and the dimensionality of its patches is small, the score function for a noisy patch can be computed tractably using an optimal, closed-form denoiser, eliminating the need for neural network training. We integrate this patch-based denoiser into an efficient, training-free image diffusion model, and we describe how our method connects to classical patch-based image restoration techniques. Our approach achieves state-of-the-art generation quality and diversity compared to trained single-image diffusion models, and we demonstrate applications, including unconditional image generation, text-guided stylization, image symmetrization, and retargeting. Further, we show that our approach is compatible with latent space diffusion, and we show multiple additional acceleration techniques to achieve megapixel single-image generation in one second, and gigapixel generation in minutes.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 无训练的单图像扩散模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 无训练的单图像扩散模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 无训练的单图像扩散模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes a training-free diffusion model for generating images that match the patch distribution of a single reference image, using optimal closed-form denoising without neural network training.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对单图像扩散模型训练成本高的问题，本文提出无需训练的扩散模型。通过将图像建模为多尺度补丁数据集，利用最优闭式去噪器计算分数函数，避免了神经网络训练。实验表明该方法能高效生成内部结构与参考图像一致的图像，为扩散模型提供了一种轻量级替代方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对单图像扩散模型训练成本高的问题，本文提出无需训练的扩散模型。通过将图像建模为多尺度补丁数据集，利用最优闭式去噪器计算分数函数，避免了神经网络训练。实验表明该方法能高效生成内部结构与参考图像一致的图像，为扩散模型提供了一种轻量级替代方案。

quick_skim.title: Decoupled Residual Denoising Diffusion Models for Unified and Data Efficient Image-to-Image Translation
quick_skim.abstract: We propose Decoupled Residual Denoising Diffusion models (DRDD) for unified and data-efficient image-to-image (I2I) translation. While diffusion models have advanced I2I translation in terms of quality and diversity, we uncover a previously under-explored property in diffusion models. Crucially, beyond its conventional role of manifold lifting (i.e., moving data off low-dimensional manifolds), injecting Gaussian noise facilitates domain harmonization by implicitly aligning feature distributions across domains, a property particularly advantageous for unified I2I translation. However, existing diffusion models prematurely erode this harmonization effect, as noise and residuals are simultaneously removed in a single coupled diffusion process. To address this, DRDD decouples the diffusion process into two sequential and independent diffusion stages: (1) a stochastic noise diffusion for domain harmonization and manifold lifting, and (2) a deterministic residual diffusion that learns the core semantic mapping entirely within the fixed-noise domain. This decoupling preserves harmonization and manifold lifting effects throughout the transformation, substantially simplifying the learning of unified mappings across diverse tasks and domains. Notably, the noise diffusion stage is trained exclusively on abundant, unpaired target-domain images, greatly improving data efficiency. Comprehensive theoretical and empirical analysis demonstrates that DRDD is broadly compatible with mainstream diffusion models and consistently delivers robust, unified I2I translation, even under limited paired data. Our code is available at https://github.com/HKU-HealthAI/DRDD.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes decoupled residual diffusion for unified I2I translation, applicable to video interpolation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出解耦残差扩散模型用于统一图像翻译，可扩展至视频插帧
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出解耦残差扩散模型用于统一图像翻译，可扩展至视频插帧
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出解耦残差扩散模型用于统一图像翻译，可扩展至视频插帧
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Decoupled Residual Denoising Diffusion (DRDD) for unified image-to-image translation, revealing that noise injection also facilitates domain harmonization, enabling data-efficient translation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有扩散模型进行图像到图像翻译时，噪声注入除提升多样性外，还具有域对齐作用，但现有模型过早擦除该效果。本文提出解耦残差去噪扩散模型(DRDD)，将噪声和残差分别去除，保留域对齐能力，实现统一且数据高效的图像翻译。实验表明在多种翻译任务上性能优异，为视频插帧等时序生成提供了潜在工具。

quick_skim.title: MedSyn2: Flexible Control of 3D CT Generation via Text and Semantically-Defined Segmentation Prompts
quick_skim.abstract: Generative models for volumetric medical images have found many applications in medical imaging, ranging from data augmentation to serving as priors for inverse problems. For these applications, generating high-resolution 3D images with strong controllability is essential but remains highly challenging. Existing approaches typically control generation either through radiology reports used as text prompts or through full image segmentation. While text-based prompting is flexible, it provides limited spatial control over the location, shape, and boundary of abnormalities. In contrast, segmentation-based methods receive precise spatial guidance but are restrictive in requiring full-organ annotations. In this work, we propose a flexible multimodal framework for controllable volumetric image generation that supports input from radiology reports and segmentation prompts (both optional). Our approach allows users to provide segmentation of a specific anatomy or abnormality without requiring full-organ annotations. The semantic meaning of the segmentation mask is specified through an accompanying text description, resulting in a highly flexible and scalable conditioning mechanism. We develop a memory-efficient architecture based on a modified diffusion transformer that jointly processes image and segmentation tokens. The model further incorporates gated attention to effectively attend to long radiology reports. Experiments demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art perceptual and semantic scores (e.g., 24% relative improvement in mean FID), generates high-resolution anatomically consistent CT volumes, and improves data efficiency when used for data augmentation. Radiologists' evaluation further confirms strong alignment between generated and real medical images.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: 3D CT generative model with flexible control
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MedSyn2 proposes a generative model for 3D CT with flexible control via text and segmentation prompts, enabling high-resolution and controllable volumetric image generation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: MedSyn2 提出一种生成式模型，用于高分辨率3D CT图像的灵活控制生成。现有方法要么缺乏空间控制，要么需要全器官标注。该方法通过结合文本提示和语义定义的分割提示，实现了对生成图像中异常区域的位置、形状和边界的精确控制。实验表明，该方法在保持图像质量的同时，显著提升了生成的可控性，对医学图像数据增强和逆问题求解具有重要价值。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: MedSyn2 提出一种生成式模型，用于高分辨率3D CT图像的灵活控制生成。现有方法要么缺乏空间控制，要么需要全器官标注。该方法通过结合文本提示和语义定义的分割提示，实现了对生成图像中异常区域的位置、形状和边界的精确控制。实验表明，该方法在保持图像质量的同时，显著提升了生成的可控性，对医学图像数据增强和逆问题求解具有重要价值。

quick_skim.title: Site4Drug: Predicting Drug-Binding Target Sites with an AI Agent
quick_skim.abstract: Selecting where to intervene on a protein (i.e., choosing a targetable site) is often a more ambiguous and failure-prone bottleneck than selecting what binds, especially for membrane proteins where accessibility, topology, and post-translational modifications (PTMs) constrain actionable regions. We present Site4Drug, a modality-aware site-finding agent that outputs a ranked list of targetable regions with explicit constraints, evidence summaries, risk flags, and a traceable decision log.   Rather than requiring users to specify the drug modality upfront, Site4Drug can recommend a binding modality (e.g., antibody/peptide-like vs small-molecule) from the same evidence used for site discovery, including topology, hydropathy, PTM propensity, disulfides, domain context, and sequence. Importantly, this evidence is applied consistently across modalities, including small-molecule pocket discovery, to avoid selecting chemically plausible but biologically occluded sites.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: predict drug-binding target sites, enabling ligand design
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 预测药物结合靶点，支持配体设计
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 预测药物结合靶点，支持配体设计
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 预测药物结合靶点，支持配体设计
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Site4Drug is an AI agent that predicts drug-binding target sites with explicit constraints and modality recommendations, directly supporting structure-based ligand design.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: Site4Drug 提出一个多模态感知的AI智能体，用于预测蛋白质上的可药结合位点。不同于传统仅预测位点，它能结合拓扑、翻译后修饰等证据推荐结合模态（如小分子或抗体），并输出带约束的位点排名。该方法直接服务于基于结构的配体设计，为后续分子生成提供了明确的靶点信息，显著降低了药物设计中的位点选择错误风险。

quick_skim.title: MORPHOS: Autoregressive 4D Generation with Temporal Structured Latents
quick_skim.abstract: We present MORPHOS, a novel autoregressive framework that generates dynamic 3D assets from videos across diverse representations, including meshes, 3D Gaussians, and radiance fields. Existing methods are typically limited to a single representation, struggle to model topological changes, or fail to maintain temporal consistency over long videos. To address these limitations, we introduce the Temporal Structured Latents (T-SLAT), a unified 4D representation that jointly encodes geometry and appearance along the temporal dimension. Leveraging T-SLAT, MORPHOS autoregressively generates dynamic 3D assets via causal attention, conditioning each frame on its preceding history to ensure temporal consistency while handling evolving topologies. We also propose a temporal-structural augmentation to mitigate error accumulation in autoregressive generation. MORPHOS achieves state-of-the-art performance in appearance and competitive results in geometry across multiple benchmarks, demonstrating superior generalization across various representations and robustness in long-horizon generation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: autoregressive 4D generation of dynamic 3D assets from videos
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes MORPHOS, an autoregressive framework with Temporal Structured Latents (T-SLAT) for generating dynamic 3D assets from videos, ensuring temporal consistency and handling topological changes across multiple representations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 问题：现有动态3D生成方法受限于单一表示、难以处理拓扑变化且时序一致性差。方法：提出时序结构化潜变量T-SLAT，统一编码几何与外观时序，结合因果注意力自回归生成动态3D资产。结果：在多种表示上验证，实现时序一致且支持拓扑变化的4D生成。意义：为4D内容生成提供统一框架，有望推动3D场景生成和视频生成应用。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 问题：现有动态3D生成方法受限于单一表示、难以处理拓扑变化且时序一致性差。方法：提出时序结构化潜变量T-SLAT，统一编码几何与外观时序，结合因果注意力自回归生成动态3D资产。结果：在多种表示上验证，实现时序一致且支持拓扑变化的4D生成。意义：为4D内容生成提供统一框架，有望推动3D场景生成和视频生成应用。

quick_skim.title: Programming Biomolecular Interactions with All-Atom Generative Model
quick_skim.abstract: Biomolecular interactions lie at the core of cellular life, spanning diverse molecular modalities from small molecules to nucleic acids and proteins. Nevertheless, design strategies remain separated despite shared physicochemical principles of molecular recognition. Here we present AnewOmni, a unified generative framework trained on more than 5 million biomolecular complexes, that enables transferable molecular design across molecular scales by assembling chemically meaningful building blocks at atomic resolution. We further introduce programmable graph prompts to support user-defined chemical, topological, and geometric steering during generation, exploring hybrid and unconventional chemistries beyond canonical structures. We demonstrate that transferable learning of interaction patterns and physical constraints across molecular modalities is possible, via an atom-to-block latent space capturing both atomic details and structural priors. The framework successfully designed small molecules, peptides, and nanobodies targeting the challenging KRAS G12D switch II pocket, as well as orthosteric peptides and allosteric small-molecule inhibitors for PCSK9 in the absence of known binding site, achieving 23%-75% success with only low-throughput validation, bypassing modality-specific high-throughput screening. AnewOmni is the first to succeed in functional molecular design across all scales, from small chemical entities to large biologics, and represents a stepstone towards general molecular reasoning engines, advocating a generative foundation model for biomolecular interactions to enter regimes where data and human intuition remain limited.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: all-atom generative model for programming biomolecular interactions applicable to ligand design
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 全原子生成模型可编程生物分子相互作用，适用于配体设计

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AnewOmni is a unified all-atom generative framework trained on millions of biomolecular complexes that enables transferable molecular design, including ligand generation for protein pockets.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 生物分子相互作用的设计策略往往分离，但共享物理化学原理。本文提出AnewOmni，一个统一的全原子生成框架，在超过5百万生物分子复合物上训练，通过组装化学上有意义的构建块实现跨尺度分子设计。引入可编程图提示支持用户定义的化学、拓扑和几何引导生成，展示了在蛋白质口袋中生成配体的能力，有望推动药物设计。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 生物分子相互作用的设计策略往往分离，但共享物理化学原理。本文提出AnewOmni，一个统一的全原子生成框架，在超过5百万生物分子复合物上训练，通过组装化学上有意义的构建块实现跨尺度分子设计。引入可编程图提示支持用户定义的化学、拓扑和几何引导生成，展示了在蛋白质口袋中生成配体的能力，有望推动药物设计。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 针对蛋白质口袋的配体分子生成相关论文, especially work that connects or combines: generating ligands that bind to specific protein pockets; molecule generation conditioned on protein pocket structure; diffusion models for designing ligands in protein pockets; molecular generation aware of protein target structure; generative models for protein-ligand complex prediction; generating 3D molecules that fit protein pockets; generative models for structure based ligand design targeting binding sites; methods for pocket specific molecular generation using deep learning; target aware ligand generation conditioned on protein structure.

quick_skim.title: Agentic Molecular Recovery via Molecule-Aware Exploration
quick_skim.abstract: Text-guided molecular generation with LLMs often yields invalid SMILES. We argue that invalid drafts should be addressed through a shift from validity-oriented repair to identity-preserving molecular recovery: the objective is not only to restore chemical validity, but also to preserve target-relevant structural cues and recover the molecular identity implied by the description. This perspective reveals the limitations of existing correction strategies. Post-hoc repair can recover validity while distorting key structures, LLM-only correction can introduce unintended global drift, and generic agentic correction remains constrained by greedy single-candidate trajectories even when equipped with executable RDKit edit tools. To address these limitations, we propose AMREC, which couples molecule-aware mismatch tracking with expanded candidate exploration and trajectory-level selection. On invalid ChEBI-20 drafts from three backbone models, AMREC achieves the strongest overall recovery profile across structural, exact-match, and string-level metrics.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: molecular recovery from invalid SMILES using agentic exploration
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过智能体探索从无效SMILES恢复分子
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过智能体探索从无效SMILES恢复分子
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过智能体探索从无效SMILES恢复分子
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: AMR transforms invalid SMILES into valid molecules while preserving structural cues by molecule-aware exploration with multiple candidates.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: LLM生成分子常产生无效SMILES，现有修复方法可能扭曲关键结构。本文提出AMR框架，将问题从有效性修复转为身份保持的分子恢复，通过分子感知探索保留目标结构的分子记忆。该方法同时考虑多个候选并进行RDKit约束编辑。实验表明，AMR在保持分子特性的同时恢复有效性，优于后修复和LLM纠正。该工作为分子生成提供更可靠的修复策略。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: LLM生成分子常产生无效SMILES，现有修复方法可能扭曲关键结构。本文提出AMR框架，将问题从有效性修复转为身份保持的分子恢复，通过分子感知探索保留目标结构的分子记忆。该方法同时考虑多个候选并进行RDKit约束编辑。实验表明，AMR在保持分子特性的同时恢复有效性，优于后修复和LLM纠正。该工作为分子生成提供更可靠的修复策略。

quick_skim.title: Towards Generalizable Protein-ligand Co-folding with ACER
quick_skim.abstract: Predicting protein-ligand complex structures is a central challenge in drug discovery. While recent co-folding models such as AlphaFold-3 achieve accurate structure prediction, they fail to generalize to underexplored binding interfaces - systematically misplacing ligands, particularly for allosteric or structurally novel targets. To address this gap, we present ACER (A daptive Co-folding via pocket E xploration and pose R anking), a training-free framework that (a) enables co-folding models to systematically explore alternative binding pockets, and (b) leverages the discovered pockets to increase pose accuracy. Our method enables the efficient discovery of non-prevalent pockets without prior expert knowledge. ACER improves pocket discovery and pose accuracy on allosteric targets and structurally novel complexes, successfully modeling binding interfaces that are under-represented or absent from the training set. Our results demonstrate how improved sampling dynamics enhance the generalisability of co-folding models without retraining.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 蛋白质-配体共折叠与口袋探索
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 蛋白质-配体共折叠与口袋探索
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 蛋白质-配体共折叠与口袋探索
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: ACER improves protein-ligand co-folding by exploring alternative binding pockets and ranking poses, enhancing generalization to novel interfaces.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有共折叠模型无法泛化到未知结合界面的问题，提出ACER框架，无需训练即可系统探索替代结合口袋并排序构象，显著提升口袋发现和姿态精度。该方法在预测变构或结构新颖靶点配体时表现优越，为药物发现中的结构预测提供可泛化工具。

quick_skim.title: Diff-CA: Separating Common and Salient Factors with Diffusion Models
quick_skim.abstract: Contrastive Analysis aims to separate factors that are common between two data distributions from those that are salient to only one of them. Existing contrastive methods are based on generative models (e.g., VAEs or GANs) that often suffer from limited reconstruction and image quality, which hampers effective latent factor separation and limits their applicability to high-fidelity image generation and edition. We propose a novel conditioning framework for diffusion models that enables contrastive decomposition without compromising generation quality. We first train a prompt-free, image-conditioned diffusion model, and then learn to decompose the conditioning into a common and a salient factor, using weak supervision. We prove that the additive contrastive factorization, commonly assumed in prior work, is identifiable under mild conditions. This factorization enables targeted operations by swapping or interpolating only the salient factor.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: diffusion model for contrastive decomposition, applicable to multimodal learning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 扩散模型用于对比分解，适用于多模态学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 扩散模型用于对比分解，适用于多模态学习
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 扩散模型用于对比分解，适用于多模态学习
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Diff-CA uses diffusion models with weak supervision to separate common and salient factors, enabling high-fidelity image generation and editing.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出Diff-CA，一种基于扩散模型的对比分解框架。通过无提示的图像条件扩散模型和弱监督学习，有效分离公共因子和显著因子，在保持生成质量的同时实现高保真图像编辑。该方法解决了现有VAE/GAN方法重建质量差的问题。

quick_skim.title: Vibe Coding Specificity Foundation Models
quick_skim.abstract: Molecular recognition - the determination of which agent binds which target - governs adaptive immunity, gene regulation, signal transduction, RNA silencing, enzyme catalysis, and the selectivity of therapeutics. Determining binding specificity remains dependent on experimental screening or domain-specific computational tools that do not generalize across binding modalities. Transformer softmax attention is mathematically identical to the Boltzmann distribution governing molecular binding. This identity, together with five conditions of molecular recognition systems, prescribes a single neural network architecture for cross-modal binding prediction: dual sequence encoders, symmetric contrastive learning, and a learned physical temperature. A Specificity Foundation Model (SFM) is an instance of this physics-derived, sequence-to-sequence architecture that maps any agent-target sequence pair to a binding compatibility score, enabling bidirectional retrieval across molecular recognition domains without requiring structural information. The first SFM for antibody-antigen binding demonstrated ~100,000-fold greater data efficiency than comparable vision-language models. Here we report six SFMs across six molecular recognition domains - transcription factor-DNA, enzyme-substrate, peptide-MHC, CRISPR gRNA-off-target genomic DNA, microRNA-mRNA target, and small molecule drug-target protein - using the identical architecture without modification and trained using publicly available data only. Evaluated by cross-modal retrieval from pools of 512 candidates (random baseline 0.2%), in-distribution R@1 ranges from 27.7% to 98.0% across the six domains. mir-SFM retrieves miRNA targets at 98.0% R@1, including the ~80% of validated interactions that seed-matching tools cannot find. mhcSFM achieves 95.4% R@1 on held-out rare HLA alleles absent from training. Applying crisprSFM to CRISPR off-target prediction improves precision to 94.0% compared to 33.2% from Hamming distance alone. All six SFMs were built by a domain expert with no programming experience using vibe coding - natural-language-directed AI coding agents - with numerical claims independently verified by an orthogonal AI auditor. These results establish SFMs as a physics-derived, sequence-native class of model that augments experimental and computational workflows across molecular recognition domains.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: foundation model for molecular binding specificity prediction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 分子结合特异性预测基础模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 分子结合特异性预测基础模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 分子结合特异性预测基础模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a Specificity Foundation Model (SFM) leveraging the equivalence between Transformer attention and Boltzmann distribution for cross-modal binding prediction, applicable to protein-ligand complexes.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 问题：分子结合特异性预测依赖实验或专用工具，缺乏通用模型。方法：利用Transformer注意力与玻尔兹曼分布的数学等同性，提出特异性基础模型SFM，采用双序列编码器和对比学习。结果：在多种结合模态上实现了泛化性能，可预测蛋白质-配体结合特异性。意义：为跨模态分子识别提供了统一的计算框架，可用于指导配体设计。
